scope: Quake Live retail netcode and adjacent message wrapper parity

rounds.focus: message wrapper layout plus netchan/XOR semantic checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with compact msg_t source reconstruction

rounds.focus: client usercmd packet writer and server parser checkpoints
server snapshot authoring, reliable resend, and send scheduling checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with documented classic-download compatibility divergence

rounds.focus: server connectionless OOB dispatch and VAC secure-mode policy checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with sv_vac hard rejection source reconstruction

rounds.focus: client connectionless challenge request and response checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with documented Steam-service unknown-OOB fallback divergence

rounds.focus: download bootstrap, workshop completion, and classic UDP autodownload compatibility checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with SV_DoneDownload active-client guard reconstruction and classic autodownload compatibility classification

rounds.focus: client server-message parsing, snapshot intake, and gamestate/systeminfo checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with CL_ParseSnapshot areamask guard and CL_ParseServerMessage bad-command diagnostic reconstruction

rounds.focus: netchan setup, OOB packet framing, and Win32 socket boundary checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with Sys_GetPacket receive-error diagnostic reconstruction

rounds.focus: address comparison, loopback queue, and NET_StringToAdr checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; NET_SendLoopPacket remains a source helper matched through the retail NET_SendPacket loopback branch

rounds.focus: qcommon MSG bitstream primitives, OOB scalar paths, and string wrapper checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified with MSG_WriteString, MSG_WriteBigString, and MSG_ReadString high-byte scrub removal reconstruction

rounds.focus: MSG_ReadData, packet-entity delta, and playerstate delta codec checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; retained Quake Live entity and playerstate delta codecs are now directly manifest-backed

rounds.focus: Huffman bit helpers, adaptive compression/decompression core, and MSG_initHuffman bootstrap checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; compressed message transport is now directly manifest-backed

rounds.focus: MSG_WriteData, short/long scalar write wrappers, and short/long scalar read wrappers
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; retained scalar data wrapper lane is now directly manifest-backed

rounds.focus: MSG_ReportChangeVectors_f debug reporter and changeVectors command registration
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; retained delta-codec debug reporter is now directly manifest-backed

rounds.focus: Quake Live retail protocol profile, command-token helpers, connect-packet guard, and feature gates
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; source-reconstructed protocol profile lane is now manifest-backed

rounds.focus: shared transport constants, netchan structure layout, and server fragment queue checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; packet sizing, ring sizing, fragmentation buffers, and queued fragment sends are now manifest-backed

rounds.focus: client/server netchan endpoint wrappers and server #462 queued-fragment dispatch
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; endpoint encode/decode gates, EOF appends, process gates, and queued-fragment drain behavior are now manifest-backed

rounds.focus: client usercmd generation and send-cadence checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; command creation, frame-msec clamping, send throttling, and CL_SendCmd orchestration are now manifest-backed

rounds.focus: server rate scheduler helper and fragment-drain cadence checkpoints
rounds.outcome: verified without source behavior changes; SV_RateMsec arithmetic and send-loop rate scheduling are now manifest-backed

behavior_checkpoints: compact 0x1c-byte retail x86 message header copied by MSG_Copy
overflowed is the first stored qboolean
oob is the second stored qboolean
no distinct allowoverflow field survives in the retail wrapper layer
data, maxsize, cursize, readcount, and bit follow the two qboolean fields
describes the active Quake Live retail network profile
stores OOB command tokens, reliable command tokens, userinfo keys, protocol numbers, and feature booleans
keeps connect and demo protocol numbers in the profile instead of hard-coding PROTOCOL_VERSION at each call site
source-level reconstruction helper rather than a retained standalone retail function
compatibility_notes: this profile abstraction is a source reconstruction layer around retail-observed constants and call-site behavior; it intentionally has no single retail function address

behavior_checkpoints: selects NETPROFILE_QL_RETAIL and name ql-retail-steam
uses retail protocol 91 for connect and demo protocol numbers
stores retail command tokens such as getchallenge, challengeResponse, connect, connectResponse, getserversResponse, download, nextdl, stopdl, and donedl
stores retail userinfo keys such as protocol, qport, challenge, sv_serverid, fs_game, and sv_pure
enables challenge handshake, client qport, netchan qport, reliable XOR, and compressed connect for the retail profile
keeps platform auth and workshop support behind build-time service gates
compatibility_notes: online-service support bits are build-gated so default offline builds preserve the repository's QL_BUILD_ONLINE_SERVICES policy

behavior_checkpoints: NET_GetProtocolProfile returns the singleton retail descriptor
NET_ProtocolName returns the descriptor name
NET_ProtocolVersion returns connectProtocol
NET_DemoProtocol returns demoProtocol
NET_ProtocolSupports accepts only the descriptor connectProtocol
compatibility_notes: grouped manifest entry for small source-reconstruction accessors rather than separate direct retail symbols

behavior_checkpoints: OOB command getters return tokens from the active profile descriptor
reliable command getters return download, pure, userinfo, and reliable-disconnect tokens from the active profile descriptor
userinfo key getters return server-browser, connect, client, and systeminfo keys from the active profile descriptor
call sites use these getters instead of hard-coded classic Quake III command strings
compatibility_notes: grouped manifest entry covers the source-reconstructed getter family because each helper is a direct descriptor-field accessor

behavior_checkpoints: exact-match predicates reject null command or profile-token pointers
exact-match predicates use case-insensitive token comparison
server-list response matching uses prefix comparison through NET_CommandStartsWithProfileToken
download, pure, userinfo, and reliable-disconnect command predicates dispatch through the active profile tokens
compatibility_notes: grouped manifest entry covers small source-reconstructed predicate wrappers tied to retail-observed command tokens

behavior_checkpoints: rejects null messages, null data, and messages shorter than the OOB marker
checks the active profile connect token at payload offset four
requires the message to contain the full connect token
accepts exact-token packets or packets delimited by null, space, quote, newline, or carriage return
guards server compressed-connect decompression before SV_ConnectionlessPacket tokenization
compatibility_notes: source reconstruction helper introduced to identify retail compressed connect OOB payloads before Huff_Decompress

behavior_checkpoints: NET_ProtocolUsesClientQport returns usesClientQport
NET_ProtocolUsesNetchanClientQport returns usesNetchanClientQport
NET_ProtocolUsesReliableXorCodec returns usesReliableXorCodec
NET_ProtocolUsesLegacyAuthorize returns usesLegacyQ3Authorize
NET_ProtocolUsesCompressedConnect returns usesCompressedConnect
NET_ProtocolSupportsPlatformAuth returns supportsPlatformAuth
qport, XOR, compressed connect, legacy authorize, and platform-auth call sites are gated through these helpers
compatibility_notes: platform-auth and workshop capability bits remain build-gated to keep online services disabled by default

behavior_checkpoints: PACKET_BACKUP is 32 and PACKET_MASK is PACKET_BACKUP minus one for client/server packet history rings
MAX_RELIABLE_COMMANDS is 64 for retained reliable command retransmit buffers
MAX_MSGLEN is 16384 for whole reliable messages and fragment assembly buffers
MAX_DOWNLOAD_WINDOW is 8 for the classic UDP download block window
client and server structures consume these constants for outPackets, snapshots, reliableCommands, downloadBlocks, frames, netchan_t, and netchan_buffer_t storage
compatibility_notes: constant group has no standalone retail address; it is verified through shared structure layout, netchan function behavior, and retained client/server ring users

behavior_checkpoints: MAX_PACKETLEN is 1400 for outgoing UDP packet scratch buffers
FRAGMENT_SIZE is MAX_PACKETLEN minus 100, matching the documented retail 0x514 fragment payload size
PACKET_HEADER remains 10 bytes for two ints and a short in the classic header accounting
FRAGMENT_BIT is the high bit of the outgoing sequence number
Netchan_Transmit fragments messages at length greater than or equal to FRAGMENT_SIZE and rejects messages larger than MAX_MSGLEN
Netchan_Process strips FRAGMENT_BIT, reads fragment start and length, assembles in-order fragments, and only releases a complete message when fragmentLength is less than FRAGMENT_SIZE
compatibility_notes: constant group has no standalone retail address; it is verified through retained Netchan_Transmit, Netchan_TransmitNextFragment, and Netchan_Process behavior

behavior_checkpoints: stores socket identity, dropped packet count, remote address, qport, incoming sequence, and outgoing sequence
stores incoming fragment sequence and length with a MAX_MSGLEN fragmentBuffer
stores outgoing unsentFragments state, unsentFragmentStart, unsentLength, and a MAX_MSGLEN unsentBuffer
Netchan_Setup zeroes the structure, assigns the socket, remote address, qport, incoming sequence 0, and outgoing sequence 1
clientConnection_t and server client_t each embed one netchan_t
compatibility_notes: structure row has no standalone retail address; field layout is verified through retained netchan setup, transmit, process, and client/server embedding evidence

behavior_checkpoints: server-side queued fragmented sends store a msg_t and MAX_MSGLEN backing buffer
queue nodes link through next
server client_t stores netchan_start_queue and netchan_end_queue for paced fragmented sends
server client_t retains one netchan_t alongside the queued-fragment send list
compatibility_notes: server queue structure has no standalone retail address; it is verified as the retained slow-link companion to the netchan fragment path
